---
title: "Teaching Profile"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.profile-header}

![](profile.jpg){.profile-photo-small}

::: {.profile-header-text}
## Ittisak Promma, Ph.D.

Chemical engineering educator and computational researcher working in process modeling, simulation, thermodynamics, optimization, and hybrid modeling.
:::

:::

## Education

**Ph.D. in Chemical Engineering**  
University of Waterloo, Canada

**M.Eng. in Chemical Engineering**  
Chemical Engineering Practice School, King Mongkut's University of Technology Thonburi, Thailand

**B.Eng. in Chemical Engineering**  
King Mongkut's University of Technology Thonburi, Thailand

## Teaching interests

- Process modeling and simulation
- Thermodynamics
- Numerical methods for chemical engineering
- Process optimization and control
- Hybrid modeling and machine learning
- Computational thinking for process systems engineering

## Research interests

- Process systems engineering
- Bioprocess modeling
- CFD-informed compartment models
- Dynamic flux balance analysis
- Population balance modeling
- Physics-informed machine learning

## Teaching philosophy

My teaching emphasizes the connection between equations, physical meaning, and computational implementation. I want students to see process models not only as mathematical exercises, but also as practical tools for understanding, designing, and improving chemical engineering systems.

## Contact

**Email:** [itpromma@outlook.com](mailto:itpromma@outlook.com)
